name: Sundowner's Yellowtail Amandine
recipeInstructions:
Cook fish: Lightly dust the filets with flour. In a shallow dish, beat eggs well. Spread almonds evenly in another similar dish. Heat oil in a medium-size skillet over medium-high heat. Dredge filets in egg wash, placing them on almonds and coating both sides. Immediately place filets in preheated skillet and cook for 3 minutes. Turn filets over and cook an additional 2 minutes, or until done. The coating should be golden brown but not burned. Remove filets from skillet and cover to keep warm. Make sauce: In a small saucepan, melt butter over medium-high heat. When butter is bubbly and slightly brown, add brown sugar and stir until blended. Reduce heat and add amaretto. Stir and cook for 3 minutes, until the sauce thickens. Pour the sauce over the filets.
Nutritional info per serving: 795 calories; 52g protein, 26g carbohydrates, 51g fat (57%), 3.8g fiber, 254mg cholesterol, 282mg sodium
